name: Hiligaynon

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Hiligaynon (hili1240).

notes: Skeleton. A Central Philippine (Bisayan) Austronesian language of Panay and Negros, written in a Latin alphabet based on the modern Filipino/abakada convention. The grapheme map covers the native consonant and five-vowel inventory plus the digraph ng for the velar nasal. The phonemic glottal stop (word-initial, intervocalic, and word-final) is not written in the standard orthography and is left unencoded, as are contrastive stress and vowel length, so a nonzero PER floor is expected. The mid vowels e and o largely reflect Spanish loans and later mergers; e is mapped to a lax value. Spanish/English loan letters (c, f, j, ñ, q, v, x, z) are omitted from the native map.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Hiligaynon (hili1240)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
